data.title: PTA-News: 4Science SpA: 4Science Shareholders Meeting approves with 100% of the attendees the 2024 Full Year Report and a capital increase
data.description:
4Science SpA

by Massimo Grosso

Business news for the stock market Milano (pta/05.06.2025/15:45) - 4Science Shareholders Meeting approves with 100% of the attendees the 2024 Full Year Report and a capital increase of up to a maximum of Euro 5 million (end) emitter: 4Science SpA address: Viale Achille Papa 30, 20149 Milano country: Italy contact person: Massimo Grosso phone: +39 327 9031218 e-mail: massimo.grosso74@gmail.com website: www.4science.com ISIN(s): IT0005503989 (share) stock exchanges: Vienna MTF in Vienna pressetext Nachrichtenagentur GmbH Ad-hoc and financial news distributed by pressetext.adhoc. data.title: Verint Price Target Maintained With a $30.00/Share by Wedbush
Ireland's Economy Grew Much Faster Than Expected on Tariff Stockpiling — Update
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

Ireland's economy grew three times as rapidly as first estimated in the first quarter, driven by stockpiling of pharmaceuticals and other goods by U.S. businesses ahead of threatened tariffs.

The huge revision in output announced by the country's statistics agency Thursday means the eurozone economy likely grew much faster than previously estimated, with new figures for growth in the three months through March to be released Friday.

In a news conference Thursday, ECB President Christine Lagarde said an upgrade in the growth estimate for the eurozone is likely, referring to Ireland without naming the country.

"It's the buildup in inventories in fear and anticipation of tariffs," she said. "I would not exclude that the number will be revised up given the volume of trade we are seeing out of some countries in particular."

However, activity is likely to slow as the year advances and the need to add to stocks of pharmaceuticals diminishes, while the higher U.S. tariffs on most other goods that have been imposed over recent months will hit Ireland and other parts of Europe.

Ireland's Central Statistics Office said the country's gross domestic product was 9.7% higher in the first quarter than in the final three months of 2024, compared with an initial growth estimate of 3.2%.

"It's clear that the front-running of U.S. tariffs gave a huge boost to Ireland's GDP," wrote Jack Allen-Reynolds, an economist at Capital Economics, in a note to clients.

It isn't clear how long the stockpiling of drugs will last. While the Trump administration announced increases in tariffs on a wide range of goods in April, pharmaceuticals were exempted.

However, the administration has launched an investigation under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962, which allows the president to impose tariffs on goods deemed essential for national security.

Higher tariffs would make pharmaceuticals more expensive for U.S. importers. Since many drugs can be stored for a period of time, it makes sense for U.S. buyers to build stocks ahead of possible tariffs as a way of keeping costs, and prices for patients, down.

Although Ireland only accounted for 3.5% of total output in the eurozone last year, an increase of almost 10% in its GDP will have delivered a significant boost to growth in the currency area.

The European Union's statistics agency last estimated that the currency area's GDP increased by 0.3% in the first quarter, but Allen-Reynolds estimated that would likely be revised to 0.6% when new figures are released Friday.

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: Brown-Forman Down Over 15%, on Track for Record Percent Decrease — Data Talk
data.description:
Brown-Forman Corporation Class B (BF.B) is currently at $28.20, down $5.01 or 15.08%

  • Would be lowest close since Oct. 15, 2013, when it closed at $27.90
  • Would be largest percent decrease on record (Based on available data back to Jan. 21, 1972)
  • Currently down four of the past five days
  • Currently down two consecutive days; down 15.1% over this period
  • Worst two day stretch since the two days ending March 23, 2020, when it fell 17.75%
  • Down 25.76% year-to-date
  • Down 65.54% from its all-time closing high of $81.82 on Nov. 24, 2020
  • Down 35.80% from 52 weeks ago (June 6, 2024), when it closed at $43.92
  • Down 43.09% from its 52-week closing high of $49.54 on Oct. 17, 2024
  • Would be a new 52-week closing low
  • Traded as low as $27.19; lowest intraday level since Oct. 9, 2013, when it hit $26.90
  • Down 18.1% at today's intraday low; largest intraday percent decrease since Sept. 19, 2008, when it fell as much as 19.13%
  • Worst performer in the S&P 500 today

All data as of 9:43:16 AM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: BTCC Exchange Enhances Demo Trading Feature with Self-Service Virtual Funding and Reward System to Support Crypto Beginners
data.description:
A Media Snippet accompanying this announcement is available in this link.

VILNIUS, Lithuania, June 05, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — BTCC, a leading cryptocurrency trading platform with 14 years of secure operations, has launched significant upgrades to its demo trading feature designed to better serve cryptocurrency beginners. The enhanced upgrade now allows users to independently top up their virtual funds while earning real rewards through completing various demo trading tasks.

The upgraded demo trading feature introduces two key improvements that reflect BTCC's commitment to making cryptocurrency trading more accessible. Users can now top up virtual funds by themselves, accessing up to 500,000 USDT per week without requiring approvals. This self-service feature allows traders to instantly access more funds, giving new users more opportunities to practice trading.

Additionally, users can now complete demo trading tasks to earn trading fund rewards, which can be used to open positions in live trading. This innovative approach bridges the gap between demo and real trading, helping beginners transition more confidently while reducing their initial trading costs.

"We recognized that learning crypto trading requires extensive practice, and while our previous 100,000 USDT virtual balance was already generous compared to other exchanges, we wanted to go further," said Alex, Head of Operations at BTCC Exchange. "By allowing users to self-manage their virtual funds and earn real trading rewards, we're empowering beginners to gain the comprehensive experience they need to succeed in live trading."

Dan Liu, CEO of BTCC, reinforced the platform's commitment to user education: "At BTCC, we believe that true crypto adoption begins with understanding. This upgraded demo trading feature lowers the barrier to entry and gives new users the freedom to explore, learn, and grow—at their own pace, without pressure."

To celebrate the feature upgrade, BTCC is hosting a special Trade Your First Win demo trading competition on its official X account, featuring prizes including Amazon Gift Cards and additional bonuses.

This upgrade reinforces BTCC's mission to make crypto trading reliable and accessible to everyone. Since its establishment in 2011, BTCC has maintained an unmatched security record with zero security breaches over 14 years of operations. The platform continues to support beginners through comprehensive guides, copy trading feature, and extensive educational resources.

The enhanced demo trading feature is now available to all BTCC users, further solidifying the platform's position as a beginner-friendly cryptocurrency exchange focused on security, accessibility, and user education.

About BTCC Exchange

Founded in 2011, BTCC is one of the world's longest-serving cryptocurrency exchanges, offering secure and user-friendly trading services to millions of users globally. With a commitment to security, innovation, and community building, BTCC continues to be a trusted platform in the evolving cryptocurrency landscape.

Website: https://www.btcc.com/en-US

X: https://x.com/BTCCexchange

Contact: press@btcc.com

data.title: Penumbra Launches US FDA-Cleared Ruby XL Coil System
data.description:
Penumbra said Thursday that it has received US Food and Drug Administration clearance and has launched the Ruby XL System, a new coil platform for vascular embolization procedures.

The company said the products are intended to help physicians reduce radiation exposure while also improving vessel occlusion.

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1330 GMT on Thursday:

** Australian MGA ATC Insurance Solutions has agreed to acquire fellow BP Marsh investee company Sterling Insurance, in a deal that increases the specialist UK insurance investor's stake in ATC to 27%.

** Plus Automation will go public in the U.S. through a merger with a blank-check firm in a deal that values the self-driving truck startup at $1.2 billion, the companies said.

** Vermilion Energy will sell its United States assets for C$120 million ($87.88 million), the Canadian gas producer said, using the proceeds to repay debt and completing its exit from the U.S. market.

** Monarch E&S Insurance Services has acquired MGA and wholesale broker Market Finders, with the addition of the Albuquerque, New Mexico-based firm expanding its reach across the Southwest.

** The founding firms of India's Bajaj Finserv <BJFS.NS> will sell at least a 1.58% stake they own in the financial services company in a deal worth 47.5 billion rupees ($554 million), news channel ET NOW reported, citing sources.

** Empiric Student Property <ESP.L> said it has received a buyout proposal worth 710.6 million pounds ($964.8 million) from Unite Group <UTG.L>, sending shares of the British real estate investment trust up more than 6%.

** Kimberly-Clark <KMB.N> struck a deal with Brazilian pulp maker Suzano <SUZB3.SA> to sell a majority stake in its international tissue business, valuing the business at about $3.4 billion, the Kleenex tissue maker said.

** South Korea's Hanwha Systems <272210.KS> said it was selling its entire 5.4% stake in Eutelsat <ETL.PA> for about 78 million euros ($85 million), as the Franco-British satellite operator seeks new investors.

** Chemical maker LyondellBasell said it is in exclusive talks with Munich-based investment firm AEQUITA for the sale of certain olefin and polyolefin assets in Europe.

** British outsourcer Mitie Group <MTO.L will acquire smaller peer Marlowe <MRLM.L> for about 366 million pounds ($496 million) in a cash-and-stock deal, the companies said, sending the latter's shares up nearly 8%.

** Prada <1913.F> said it acquired a 10% stake in Italian leather group Rino Mastrotto, as the luxury group aims to tighten its grip over the production process.

data.title: Fueling the Future of Finance: Bectran Launches a Mission-Critical AR Command Center
data.description:
CHICAGO, June 05, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Bectran, Inc., the connected intelligence platform for end-to-end credit, collections, and AR management, has introduced a new Accounts Receivable (AR) Homepage — designed to meet the demands of modern receivable specialists who need clarity, speed, and control at every turn.

The new homepage brings real-time visibility, task urgency, and risk signals into a single, centralized view. Built as a command post, it empowers finance professionals with the infrastructure necessary to act quickly, prioritize what matters, and stay ahead of risk.

“We didn’t set out to redesign a screen — we set out to make accounts receivable more deliberate,” said Ali Kidwai, Bectran’s Head of Product & Engineering. “This homepage turns data into direction. It cuts the clutter, flags the risks, and sharpens focus on the tasks that matter most — helping every user operate with the urgency and precision today’s AR demands.”

An Interface Designed for Action

In today’s environment, the cost of disjointed systems and delayed action compounds quickly. Teams are often forced to work across various interfaces, pulling reports from one place, resolving disputes in another, and managing follow-ups through separate tools. As a result, critical insights are missed, handoffs are delayed, and the urgency of collections and approvals becomes overwhelming to manage at scale.

To address these operational blind spots, Bectran has consolidated key receivable workflows into a single command center, providing clarity to both the state of the portfolio and the actions it demands. The AR Homepage presents two primary views: a live dashboard of portfolio activity, and a task feed organized by urgency. Performance signals — collections, claims, payments, order holds — are displayed alongside time-sensitive actions such as account reviews, failed payments, and pending approvals.

By combining operational metrics with task-level visibility, teams gain immediate context without switching views or piecing together information from disparate systems. Each element is structured to reduce manual legwork in the decision-making process — whether releasing a hold, resolving a dispute, or identifying risk in the portfolio.

"Rather than building another tool, we created a data-driven workspace that helps AR teams act faster and smarter,” said Louis Ifeguni, CEO of Bectran. “It streamlines decision-making without sacrificing control.”

About Bectran 

Bectran is the creator of Intelligent CreditOps — an enterprise-grade solution that modernizes the core of credit, collections, and receivables. While most finance tools are fragmented or retrofitted, Bectran offers a unified foundation purpose-built to automate routine and deeply analytical processes, connect real-time data, and scale credit operations with confidence.

Trusted by finance teams at every stage of growth — from mid-market leaders to Fortune 100 enterprises — Bectran replaces manual, error-prone processes with intelligent, adaptable workflows across the order-to-cash cycle, giving companies the clarity, control, and confidence to drive growth without increasing risk.

Contact

Adam Coyner

Marketing Specialist

Bectran Inc

(888) 791-6620

PR@Bectran.com

data.title: Netgear to Buy Cybersecurity Firm Exium
data.description:
Netgear said in a regulatory filing Thursday that it has agreed to buy cybersecurity company Exium for less than $15 million in cash.

The company said it expects to fund the acquisition from existing reserves. The transaction is not expected to have a material impact on its operations or financial position this year, according to Netgear.

The deal is expected to close by the end of Q2 and add an integrated Secure Access Service Edge, or SASE, platform to its offerings, Netgear said.

Shares of Netgear were up more than 1% in early trading Thursday.

data.title: Market Talk Roundup: Latest on U.S. Politics
data.description:
Market Talks covering the impact of U.S. Politics and White House policies on companies and markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0936 ET - European assets are expected to benefit from a shift away from U.S. holdings as the European Central Bank likely continues cutting rates, J.P. Morgan Private Bank's Madison Faller says. J.P. Morgan Private Bank sees the scope for cuts toward a terminal deposit rate of 1.5%, the global investment strategist says. "This supports our belief that Europe can offer a worthy landing point for global investors looking to diversify away from U.S. assets," she says. European real yields remain supportive, corporate earnings show promise ahead, and Germany's fiscal package signals future growth, she adds. The ECB cut interest rates by 25 basis points, lowering the deposit rate to 2.00%. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0855 ET - Trump's tariffs will weigh on the European economy, leading to another cut to interest rates by the European Central Bank, Fitch Ratings' Charles Seville writes. The central bank cuts its key interest rate to 2.0%, and appears, Seville says, sanguine about the state of price inflation in the eurozone. Still, the growth outlook is darkened by tariffs, which represent a hit to European exports, Seville says. "We think the tariff hit to the economy will prompt at least one more cut," he says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0838 ET - The European Central Bank rate cut gives no clear signals about the future path of policy, Capital Economics' Jack Allen-Reynolds says. Recent comments suggest the ECB is at or close to the end of its loosening cycle. For that reason, July's policy decision is in the balance, he says. "While we've penciled in another cut for that meeting, it wouldn't be surprising if the bank paused," he notes. Rather than describing the disinflation process as "on track", the ECB's accompanying release acknowledges that inflation is around the 2% target. But it also says that trade tensions would cause growth and inflation to fall below projections, implying risks to the downside, Allen-Reynolds adds. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0716 ET - Base metal prices rise, with LME three-month copper up 1.3% at $9,773.50 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum up 0.1% at $2,489 a ton. Copper has gained on fresh supply disruption concerns, SP Angel analysts say in a note. The metal has been supported by reignited worries of a U.S. copper tariff, after President Trump doubled steel and aluminum import levies to 50% on Wednesday. Already tight physical supplies have been pressured by supply disruptions in the Congo, as seismic activity reduced Ivanhoe's Kamoa-Kakula copper mine operations, SP Angel says. Copper miner Teck Resources has also announced production setbacks in Chile, further hampering supply, SP Angel says. LME copper stockpiles have shrunk 75% this year, to 66,300 tons. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0659 ET - Deep reform of the World Trade Organization is long overdue, the European Union's top trade negotiator Maros Sefcovic says. The system needs to "urgently" adapt to meet the current geopolitical climate, Sefcovic says at a conference in Brussels on Thursday. Sefcovic's comments come after he met with counterparts at the OECD 2025 Ministerial Council Meeting in Paris this week. Those he met included China's Wang Wentao and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer. "We need fresh perspectives on multiple fronts," he says. That includes with China, he says, but adds that both sides need to tackle issues like overcapacity, subsidies, market access barriers, critical minerals export restrictions, investment conditions and trade diversion. (edith.hancock@wsj.com)

0630 ET - Rising U.S. long-term Treasury yields could weaken the valuation of risky assets, Credit Mutuel Asset Management's François Rimeu says in a note. The U.S. 30-year Treasury yields are up around 35 basis points since the start of April, Tradeweb data shows, due to growing fiscal concerns. Tariffs-related risks are also likely to affect the performance of risky assets like corporate bonds and equities, he says. "A bit of caution is well warranted before the summer break." (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0616 ET - Yields on short- and long-dated U.S. Treasurys are expected to move in opposite direction near term, says Principal Asset Management's Seema Shah in a note. "With bond vigilantes circling, markets are likely to demand spending cuts that are large enough or economic growth that is strong enough to bring the deficit under control," the chief global strategist says. This suggests that long-term Treasury yields are likely to remain elevated over the near term, she says. "By contrast, the slowing economy and the prospect of Federal Reserve rate cuts later in 2025 should maintain downward pressure on short-term Treasury yields," Shah says. The two-year Treasury yield is up 0.4 basis point at 3.881%, while the 10-year yield is down about 1 basis point at 4.353%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0549 ET - The U.S. bond market risks disruption from any negative developments, Credit Mutuel Asset Management's François Rimeu says in a note. The projected increase in government borrowing raises that risk, Rimeu says. "This would have adverse consequences on all risky asset classes, which is why this scenario is of great concern for us," he says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0520 ET - Volatility on the U.S. Treasury market is declining but next week's auctions could reignite stress in the bond market, ING's Chris Turner says in a note. The U.S. Treasury is scheduled to auction three-year notes on Tuesday, 10-year notes on Wednesday and 30-year bonds on Thursday. ING will also be looking out for whether political pressure on the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates increases the term premium on Treasurys--the extra yield investors demand for holding longer-dated bonds--, the global head of markets says. "That would be a dollar negative," he says. The end of Fed Chair Powell's tenure in May next year could be a difficult time for both bond markets and the dollar, Turner says. The 10-year Treasury yield is last down 1 basis point at 4.353%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0509 ET - Base metal prices are mixed, with LME three-month copper up 0.3% at $9,681.50 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 0.3% at $2,478.50 a ton. Metal prices have been volatile over the week as trade and tariff fears were reignited as the U.S. doubled steel and aluminum import tariffs. Copper has remained more resilient than other base metals as physical supplies remain tight, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. Copper inventories are changing, with metal flowing out of LME copper warehouses and instead being pulled to other exchanges in the U.S. and China, RBC writes. Fees charged by smelters to miners have fallen to their lowest level since RBC began tracking in early 2021, likely reflecting low copper concentrate supplies. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0421 ET - Fevertree Drinks keeping guidance despite the 10% U.S. tariffs on U.K. exports is a reassuringly positive message, Panmure Liberum analysts Anubhav Malhotra and Wayne Brown say in a note. The company will share the cost of tariffs equally with Molson Coors as part of their partnership arrangement as they work to mitigate their short-term effect. "A positive message as we believe tariffs could have had an unmitigated impact of as high as around 3 million pounds in 2025, equivalent to around 80bps of margin for the group," the analysts say. Shares are down 0.2% at 882 pence, and are up 31% in the year-to-date. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0400 ET - Gold futures slip but hold near monthly highs. Futures are down 0.1% at $3,394.90 a troy ounce, with prices trading in a narrow range. The precious metal had rallied in the prior session as weaker-than-expected U.S. economic data raised hopes for interest-rate cuts, ANZ Research analysts say in a note. Lower hiring and a contraction in U.S. services activity have raised expectations of monetary policy loosening to avoid a recession. Lower rates typically benefit non-interest bearing bullion. At the same time, safe-haven demand for gold has also been prominent, ANZ says. The U.S.'s relations with China and the European Union have continued to sour as President Trump doubles steel and aluminum tariffs to 50%, ANZ says. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: Further ECB Rate Cuts to Support Shift to European Assets from U.S. — Market Talk
data.description: European assets are expected to benefit from a shift away from U.S. holdings as the European Central Bank likely continues cutting rates, J.P. Morgan Private Bank's Madison Faller says. J.P. Morgan Private Bank sees the scope for cuts toward a terminal deposit rate of 1.5%, the global investment strategist says. "This supports our belief that Europe can offer a worthy landing point for global investors looking to diversify away from U.S. assets," she says. data.title: Export Slowdown Widens Canada's Trade Deficit in April, Says TD
data.description:
Canada's trade deficit widened from a revised $2.3 billion in March to $7.1 billion in April, posting the widest monthly trade deficit on record, said TD after Thursday's data.

Merchandise exports cratered (-10.8% month over month), continuing their slide from February and March's much smaller declines, noted the bank. The drop in exports was broad-based, falling in 10 of 11 industries.

Exports of passenger cars and light trucks (-22.9% month over month) heavily weighed on headline readings as buyers stockpiled in the months preceding auto tariffs, pointed out TD. Sizeable decreases were also seen in exports of consumer goods (-15.4% month over month), energy products (-7.9%), and industrial machinery and equipment (-22.5%).

Merchandise imports were down by a more moderate 3.5% month over month in April. Once again, imports of motor vehicles and parts (-17.7% month over month) contributed most to the decline amid the implementation of tariffs. Industrial machinery, equipment, and parts (-9.5%), consumer goods (-4.2%) and electronics (-5.5%) weighed on the total import reading.

In volume terms, merchandise exports were down by a hefty 9.1% month over month while imports moved lower by 2.9%.

Canada's merchandise trade surplus with the United States shrunk to its smallest point since end-2020, currently at $3.6 billion as of April.

It was an "abysmal" month for Canadian trade but one that comes as no surprise, stated the bank. April marked the first month that Canada faced the full suite of U.S. tariffs, most notably auto tariffs, which are imposing a massive headwind on auto trade.

It is encouraging to see exports to non-U.S. markets ramp up over the past two months, but it hasn't been enough to offset the sizeable retraction in shipments the the U.S, added TD.

A pull-forward in trade helped fuel healthy export gains in Q1, making a notable contribution to total Canadian gross domestic product growth. While Canada only has one trade data point on the quarter, this story is due for a significant course-correction, with net trade pointing to a significant drag on Q2 growth, according to the bank.

Risks to the near-term trade picture are also tilted to the downside. Canada recently paused many U.S. counter tariffs, which may buffer the impact on near-term imports, while exports are expected to dampen amid ongoing trade tensions.

data.title: Engie SA Target Raised to EUR22 From EUR19 by Barclays
Engie SA Raised to Overweight From Equalweight by Barclays
BioStock: SynAct Pharma refines development strategy and extends financial runway through 2026
data.description:
On June 4, SynAct Pharma hosted a Capital Markets Day to present its refined development strategy, including the launch of development initiatives in polymyalgia rheumatica. On the same day, the company announced a directed share issue of approximately SEK 37 million, along with a SEK 30 million credit facility – together securing a financial runway through the end of 2026.

Read the article at biostock.se

https://biostock.se/2025/06/synact-pharma-refines-development-strategy-and-extends-financial-runway-through-2026/

data.title: Churchill Capital IX, Plus Automation to Merge in Deal Valued at $1.2 Billion
data.description:
Churchill Capital IX and Plus Automation said Thursday they will merge in a deal that values the autonomous trucking software company at $1.2 billion ahead of its planned market debut.

The transaction is expected to provide about $300 million in gross proceeds, which is expected to fully fund Plus through the planned 2027 commercial launch of its factory-installed self-driving truck software, according to the companies.

The combined entity will operate as a publicly listed company named PlusAI following the merger, which is expected to close in Q4, the companies said.

All existing Plus shareholders and the Churchill sponsor have agreed to lock-up provisions, and no Plus shareholder will receive cash as part of the deal, they added.

data.title: NevGold Edging Up As Adds "More Significant" Oxide Gold-Antimony Results at its Limousine Butte Project in Nevada
data.description:
NevGold was early Thursday edging up, and closer to 52 week highs, after it overnight Wednesday said it discovered "further significant" oxide gold-antimony drill results at its Limousine Butte Project in Nevada.

The company added it continues to unlock the "substantial" gold-antimony potential of the project, highlighting its "promising prospects" for further exploration and development in Nevada, one of the world's prolific mining jurisdictions.

Among highlights in most recent results, the company cited 7.04 G/T AuEq over 15.2 meters (6.37 G/T Au And 0.15% antimony) within 4.14 G/T AuEq over 29.0 meters (3.56 G/T Au And 0.13% antimony), and said it expanded gold-antimony mineralization over 400 meters along strike.

"These results have some of the highest oxide gold-antimony grades that we have seen to date at Limo Butte," said NevGold Chief Executive Brandon Bonifacio.

"We have also started to expand the mineralization footprint at Resurrection Ridge, with over 800 meters defined, and we still have over 30 holes to release prior to commencing our 2025 drill program. We are continuing to see exceptional oxide gold-antimony results across a large area at the project, and we will remain focused on adding to the mineralization footprint and filling in some of the gaps in the gold-antimony geological database, with the goal of rapidly advancing Limo Butte to an initial gold-antimony Mineral Resource Estimate in 2025.

"Our metallurgical testwork program continues to advance to outline the optimal flowsheet to recover the gold and antimony, and we are expecting results over the coming weeks. The environment is optimal to continue to advance and unlock the gold-antimony potential at Limo Butte as there is a clear commitment from the United States to advance high-quality, domestic, mineral projects."

data.title: Amber, DeFi Development Sign MoU for Expansion of Solana Market Access, Treasury Services
data.description:
Amber International and DeFi Development said Thursday that they have signed a memorandum of understanding to collaborate on expanding Solana market access and treasury services.

Financial details of the partnership were not disclosed.

The companies said they will expand Amber's product offerings to provide institutional clients with access to Solana-linked investment opportunities, as well as DeFi's Solana accumulation strategy.

Amber may also serve as a broker for DeFi's treasury acquisitions, the parties said.

Amber's stock rose 3.7% in recent Thursday trading, while DeFi's shares fell 4.5%.

data.title: Beiersdorf to Create 200 Jobs at Polish Factory With $343 Million Investment
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Beiersdorf will create 200 new jobs at a Polish factory as part of a roughly $343 million investment to double local production capacity.

The German personal-care products maker said Thursday that the 300 million euro ($342.6 million) investment will double production capacity at the factory in Poznan to 500 million units.

The investment will make the Poznan site one its most strategically important and innovative plants, the company said.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Telomir Says Lead Candidate Telomir-1 Restored Gene Regulation in Animal Model of Werner Syndrome
data.description:
Telomir Pharmaceuticals said Thursday new preclinical data showed that its lead candidate Telomir-1 reset "cellular aging markers in a validated animal model of Werner Syndrome," a rare disorder characterized by premature aging.

The company said the preclinical data also showed Telomir-1 reversed body weight and muscle loss in the animal model.

The latest findings confirmed previously reported data from a preclinical study, which showed that Telomir-1 "restored lifespan and normalized physiological decline in animals with a wrn gene mutation," Telomir said.

data.title: TSX opens higher on US-China trade optimism
data.description:
Canada's main stock index opened higher on Thursday, mirroring Wall Street peers, as U.S.-China talks boosted trade deal optimism and investors awaited key job data.

At 9:32 a.m. ET (1331 GMT), the Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX composite index was up 0.3% at 26,415.03 points.
